steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage explains that HILDA is expensive because it is a longitudinal study tracking economic well-being trends that take a long time to develop, requiring sustained funding over many years. The correct choice must match this specific reason for the high cost.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — marketing studies are not likely to require data from very many individuals.: discusses marketing studies or a different context unrelated to why longitudinal economic research is costly. ✗
  • Choice B — data from 25,000 people may not be enough to reveal significant trends in economic well-being.: questions the sample size, which is not the reason given in the passage for the expense. ✗
  • Choice C — long-term trends in economic well-being are hard to find without collecting data over many years.: states that long-term trends in economic well-being cannot be identified without collecting data over many years — directly matching the passage's explanation of why sustained funding is needed. ✓
  • Choice D — long-term trends in marketing can be found without examining many years of data.: claims trends can be observed quickly, which contradicts the passage's reasoning. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
